title: Introducing Yourself
description: Learn to say your name, where you're from, and 'nice to meet you'

data.grammar_note: Jmenuju se (colloquial) or Jmenuji se (formal). Both are correct for A1.
Jsem z + genitive of city. Prahy is genitive of Praha (covered in A2). At A1, memorize the phrase.
Těší mě = literally 'it pleases me'. Very common phrase after introducing yourself.

exercises.prompt: Arrange: 'Jmenuju se Anna. Jsem z Prahy. Těší mě.'

data.explanation: Standard self-introduction order: Name → Origin → Pleasure. This is the A1 introduction pattern.

exercises.prompt: Complete: 'My name is Tom and I'm from Brno'

data.explanation: Jmenuju se Tom (My name is Tom). Jsem z Brna (I'm from Brno). Note: 'z' + genitive — Brna is genitive of Brno.

exercises.prompt: How do you formally address a man named Novák?

data.question_en: How do you address Mr. Novák?
data.explanation: Formal address: pane + vocative of surname. Nováku is vocative of Novák. The vocative ending for masculine animate is -u.
Paní = Mrs. / Madam. Used to address married women formally: 'Dobrý den, paní Nováková.'

data.source: Jmenuju se Mahesh. Jsem z Indie. Těší mě.
accepted_answers: My name is Mahesh. I am from India. Nice to meet you.
data.grammar_note: Full self-introduction. 'Indie' genitive = 'Indie' (same form for this one).

exercises.answer_key: My name is Mahesh. I am from India. Nice to meet you.

data.note: Note the ě in Těší — softened t. First-syllable stress: JME-nu-ju, TĚ-ší.

data.target_text: Jmenuju se Mahesh. Jsem z Indie. Těší mě.
data.translation_en: My name is Mahesh. I'm from India. Nice to meet you.

exercises.answer_key: Jmenuju se Mahesh. Jsem z Indie. Těší mě.

exercises.prompt: Complete the dialogue with the correct response

data.scenario: Meeting someone new at a party

lines.speaker: stranger
lines.text: Ahoj, já jsem Petra. Jak se jmenuješ ty?

data.explanation: Informal context (ahoj, jmenuješ), so use Jmenuju se (colloquial).
